Now I can see:
- ID=10: N (light green) at [[33, 15], [33, 17], [35, 17], [35, 15]] with child ID=11 (W, white pixel)
- ID=8: c (charcoal) at [[32, 11], [32, 15], [32, 12], [33, 11], [35, 11], [36, 12], [36, 15], [36, 11]] - this is the left charcoal frame, but it has 13 pixels instead of 16, and the boundary is slightly different. This is the left charcoal frame, but it's slightly different. It seems like the green object (ID=10) is now inside the left charcoal frame (ID=8).
The green object with the white pixel is now at row 33-35, col 15-17. The left charcoal frame is at row 32-36, col 11-15. The green object is now inside the charcoal frame.
But the level is not completed yet. Let me think about what else might be needed.
The right green object (ID=6) is at [[30, 42], [30, 44], [32, 44], [32, 42]] with child ID=7 (B, black pixel). This is the right green object with the black pixel.
The right charcoal frame (ID=4) is at [[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 45], [29, 44]]. This is the right charcoal frame, but it has 15 pixels instead of 16, and the boundary is slightly different. It seems like the green object (ID=6) is overlapping with the right charcoal frame (ID=4).
So the left green object is now inside the left charcoal frame, but the right green object is still overlapping with the right charcoal frame.
Maybe the goal is to move both green objects need to be inside their respective charcoal frames. Let me try pressing DOWN again to move the right green object down to move the right green object inside the right charcoal frame.
